entry.title: 2026 NHL draft: Blackhawks could still be beneficiaries at fourth overall
entry.content: The Chicago Blackhawks were a bit unlucky with the NHL draft lottery, but they still have an excellent opportunity to select a game-changer at fourth overall in the 2026 NHL draft. Today, we look at the five best options for the Blackhawks.

entry.title: All the Money Flooding Into AI Is a Giant Warning Sign
entry.content: When companies as a group turn into sellers, it’s a reasonable sign that stocks are very overpriced.

entry.title: The Latest: Vance meets Iranian negotiators in Switzerland to work on details of deal
entry.content: U.S. and Iranian negotiators are in Switzerland for talks to work on key details of their interim agreement to end the Iran war

entry.title: Sweat, tears and camaraderie as 20,000 runners take on world’s largest ultramarathon
entry.content: For one day every June, South Africa’s searing racial inequality seems to melt away at Comrades raceIn the early morning dark, thousands of runners waited, jostling with anticipation. South Africa’s national anthem rang out. Then the haunting swell of Shosholoza, first sung by Zimbabwean migrant workers in South Africa’s goldmines. Finally, that unmistakable, spine-tingling piano: Chariots of Fire.Runners gather before the start of the marathon Continue reading...

entry.title: Cote d'Ivoire coach Emerse Faé hits out at Germany's "lack of fair play" after defeat
entry.content: Côte d'Ivoire coach Emerse Faé believed Germany showed a "lack of fair play" during Saturday's World Cup clash in Toronto, which saw his team lose 1-2.After Côte d'Ivoire defender Wilfried Singo pu...

entry.title: 'Every moment ached with importance as Northampton win compelling final'
entry.content: Northampton show their grit and unity to prevail in a tussle of a Prem final with Exeter and secure their third English title.

entry.content: Hearts set for winger signing, Celtic track forward and clubs linked with Hibernian midfielder.

entry.title: Roma and Napoli share interest in Atletico’s Molina

entry.title: Mateus Fernandes: Man United reach agreement over personal terms
entry.content: Manchester United are closing in on their second signing of the summer.Last week, Tottenham Hotspur entered the race to sign Mateus Fernandes ahead of United, with a report suggesting they are nearing...

entry.title: In Taylor Swift's beach town, every clue becomes a wedding rumor
entry.content: Rumors that Taylor Swift was planning to marry at her Rhode Island home this weekend have drawn fans, photographers and curious visitors to the seaside village of Watch Hill after a large tent appeared near her estate

entry.title: Canada’s policies force asylum seekers into US to face deportation, critics say
entry.content: Advocates say the Safe Third Country Agreement forces immigrants to head to an unsafe country: the United StatesIt was the threat of gang violence in Honduras that pushed Carlos and Antonia to flee their home. In 2021, with their toddler, Alejandro, and a handful of belongings, the married couple ventured north hoping to reach safety in the US.The journey, through Guatemala and Mexico, was filled with danger and uncertainty Continue reading...

entry.title: ‘A genuine wildlife emergency’: everything you need to know about the arrival of H5 bird flu in Australia
entry.content: What is the virus? Where did it come from? Are humans at risk? What does it mean for the agricultural sector and wildlife? Experts answer your questionsGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastA deadly strain of bird flu, known as H5 bird flu, has arrived on the Australian mainland.The federal agriculture minister, Julie Collins, this weekend confirmed that a brown skua – that had been found sick in Western Australia – had died from the H5N1 virus. The skua is a wild migratory bird. Continue reading...

entry.title: How location sharing helped police catch a serial rapist-turned-killer
entry.content: The mother of murdered model Christy Giles pleads for others to share their locations. She says the technology helped police catch David Pearce, who murdered Giles and her friend, architect Hilda Marcela Cabrales.

entry.title: "Dead Girls Don't Talk"
entry.content: A man charged with murder for poisoning two women tells a friend, "dead girls don't talk." Brave survivors speak out on their behalf. "48 Hours" contributor Jonathan Vigliotti reports.

entry.title: FAA investigates incident that forced Delta flight to abort landing
entry.content: The Federal Aviation Administration is investigating a close call between two commercial flights that forced one to abort landing in order to avoid collision

entry.title: Colombia’s runoff election expected to trigger shift in decades-long armed conflict
entry.content: Frontrunner Abelardo de la Espriella has vowed to return to full-scale military confrontation with armed groups Colombians go to the polls on Sunday in a presidential runoff expected to trigger to a dramatic shift in the country’s decades-long armed conflict, now at its most violent point since the landmark 2016 peace agreement between the government and most of the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (Farc).Polls show the frontrunner is the Trump-admiring far-right lawyer and millionaire businessman Abelardo de la Espriella, who has vowed to abandon President Gustavo Petro’s “total peace” plan of negotiating the disarmament of all criminal organisations and instead return to full-scale military confrontation with armed groups. Continue reading...

entry.title: Newsom declares state of emergency in Los Angeles for Boyle Heights warehouse fire
entry.content: Firefighters faced renewed challenges Saturday at a large Boyle Heights warehouse fire, where conditions remain highly complex as Mayor Karen Bass declared a local emergency to support response efforts.

entry.title: Timeline: Investigating the Long Island serial killings
entry.content: What began as a search for one missing woman — Shannan Gilbert — led to multiple bodies and the capture of Rex Heuermann.

entry.title: US Vice President JD Vance arrives in Switzerland to launch talks with Iran on its nuclear program
entry.content: U.S. Vice President JD Vance has landed in Switzerland to help launch negotiations with Iranian leaders over Tehran’s nuclear program

entry.title: Bolivian president declares state of emergency and deploys military to quell anti-government protests
entry.content: Bulldozers sent in to clear roadblocks that have stifled the country as farmers and Indigenous groups protest against conservative president Bolivia’s president declared a state of emergency on Saturday and deployed soldiers and bulldozers to raze anti-government roadblocks that have paralysed the country.For more than six weeks, unions, Indigenous groups and coca farmers have marched through cities and blocked roads across the country with rubble, logs and debris in protest against the conservative government. Continue reading...

entry.title: What channel is NASCAR on today? TV schedule, start time, live stream for 2026 Coronado road race
entry.content: Here's everything you need to know about the Anduril 250, including TV channel and streaming options for the NASCAR Cup Series race.

entry.title: Draymond Green admits why he did not want the OKC Thunder to win back-to-back titles
entry.content: The OKC Thunder were ready to become the first back-to-back champions since 2018, but that did not happen.

entry.title: Dallas Stars looking to move $3.25m D-Man to make room for Jason Robertson
entry.content: Dallas is shopping Ilya Lyubushkin to clear salary cap space to re-sign restricted free agent Jason Robertson to a projected $12 million extension.

entry.title: Jerry Jones’ third-round pick’s bold future prediction is exactly the attitude Cowboys love
entry.content: Shavon Revel Jr. targets elite status under Christian Parker after an injury-hit rookie year. Discover why Dallas loves his 2026 attitude.

entry.title: Dallas Stars may once again part ways with fan favorite Radek Faksa to help lock up Jason Robertson
entry.content: Dallas considers trading Radek Faksa and Ilya Lyubushkin to clear vital salary cap space for a projected $12 million extension for winger Jason Robertson.

entry.title: Small plane crashes into lake in West Milford, New Jersey
entry.content: A small plane crashed into a lake in West Milford, New Jersey, on Saturday.

entry.title: Survivors speak up to help convict man of murder, sexual assault
entry.content: David Pearce was convicted of first-degree murder for the deaths of Christy Giles and Hilda Marcela Cabrales after a night of partying in Los Angeles. He was also found guilty of raping seven other women who came forward to testify at his trial.

entry.title: The U.S. Is Ramping Up Economic Warfare. Its Enemies Aren’t Blinking.
entry.content: Washington faces a hard reality as Iran, Russia and North Korea master the art of evading U.S. sanctions.

entry.title: Fortune Cookie of the Day
entry.content:
Love isn't something you find. Love is something that finds you.
Lucky Numbers: 11, 10, 60, 1, 78, 34

entry.title: Life Hack of the Day
entry.content: When looking at recipes online, click the print link. This cuts out the unnecessary backstory and random musings.

entry.title: Useless Fact of the Day
entry.content: "Stewardesses" is the longest word typed with only the left hand.

entry.title: Quote of the Day — Zen Proverb
entry.content: “Only when you can be extremely pliable and soft can you be extremely hard and strong.” — Zen Proverb

entry.title: Vance Heads to Peace Talks, After Iran Says It Closed Strait of Hormuz
entry.content: Peace talks in Switzerland between the U.S. and Iran are on track amid renewed clashes in Lebanon between Israel and Hezbollah.

entry.content: After starting today's match on the bench, Deniz Undav scored both of Germany's goals and leads his side into the Round of 32.

entry.title: NBA trade rumor would send Giannis Antetokounmpo to Heat, $120 million All-Star to Pistons in 3-team deal
entry.content: An NBA trade rumor would send Giannis Antetokounmpo to the Miami Heat and an All-Star guard to the Detroit Pistons in a three-team trade that would send shockwaves across the league.

entry.title: Mavericks predicted to swap ex-Duke freshman phenom for $120 million Heat All-Star guard
entry.content: The Dallas Mavericks and Miami Heat were linked to a blockbuster trade involving two standout guards.

entry.title: Vice President JD Vance heads to Switzerland for Iran negotiations
entry.content: Vice President JD Vance departed for Switzerland from Joint Base Andrews for U.S.-Iran negotiations, as tensions between Israel and Lebanon remained high. Iran said Saturday that the Strait of Hormuz is closed, citing ceasefire violations after Israel continued deadly strikes in southern Lebanon overnight.

entry.content: Emergency responders arrived to find the hikers already deceased on the trails, according to the National Park Service.

entry.title: Tej Bhagra a tennis star and so much more
entry.content: Jun. 20—True story: Tej Bhagra spends most of his days at home clutching and swinging his tennis racquet. "It drives me nuts," his mother, Anjali, said with a warm smile. "He's swinging that racquet all the time." He has swung it so much and with such confidence and skill, that Bhagra — an 18-year-old who graduated from Mayo High School earlier this month — has skyrocketed all the way to a No. ...

entry.title: Century High School names baseball field after former coach Keith Kangas
entry.content: Jun. 20—ROCHESTER — The legacy of Keith Kangas in the baseball community of Rochester, and particularly at Century High School, will be recognized as the school prepares to name its field after the longtime coach. There were more than a dozen letters submitted to the school district in support of naming the field after Kangas, who coached for more than 30 years in Rochester and led Century ...

entry.title: U.S. Open: Harry Higgs forgot his pants. Don’t forget to root for him
entry.content: If you're looking for someone to root for this weekend at Shinnecock Hills, Harry Higgs is the easy choice.

entry.title: Rampant Kerry too strong for Armagh in round three
entry.content: Holders Kerry produce a ruthless display to see off Armagh 4-18 to 0-17 in round three of the All-Ireland SFC at Fitzgerald Stadium.

entry.title: Real Madrid say no contact with Bayern's Olise
entry.content: Spanish giants Real Madrid said Saturday they have not made any contact with Bayern Munich winger Michael Olise over a potential transfer."In light of the information published in various media outlets regarding an alleged interest of our club in Bayern Munich player Michael Olise, Real Madrid wishes to clarify that they have not had any direct or indirect contact with the aforementioned footballer, his representatives, or people in his circle," said Madrid in a statement.

entry.title: Russia frees 24 Filipinos after Philippine President Marcos talks with Putin
entry.content: There were reports that the Filipinos may have been victims of illegal job recruitment, Philippine officials said.

entry.title: Nobel laureate John Jumper is leaving DeepMind for rival Anthropic
entry.content: Jumper isn't the only big name leaving Google DeepMind.

entry.title: Claude Guillemot, co-founder of Ubisoft, dies in plane crash
entry.content: Claude Guillemot and a flight instructor were flying in a twin-motor Cessna 421 on Friday evening. An investigation into the crash is underway.

entry.title: A founder of Assassin's Creed maker Ubisoft killed in a plane crash in western France
entry.content: A founder of global gaming company Ubisoft, maker of Assassin's Creed, was killed in a plane crash in western France, authorities said Saturday.
